name: ubertooth
title: ubertooth
summary: 2.4 GHz wireless development platform for Bluetooth experimentation
description:
2.4 GHz wireless development platform for Bluetooth experimentation
Project Ubertooth is an open source wireless development platform suitable for
Bluetooth experimentation. This package contains everything necessary to use
the hardware dongle.
.
Ubertooth is capable of sniffing BLE (Bluetooth Smart) connections and it also
has some ability to sniff some data from Basic Rate (BR) Bluetooth Classic
connections.
.
In addition to the Bluetooth specific capabilities, there is also a simple
spectrum analyzer for the 2.4 GHz band included (ubertooth-specan-ui) which
can be used to also observe other things in this frequency band.

commands.name: ubertooth-afh
commands.description: passive detection of AFH channel map

arguments.description: passive detection of AFH channel map
arguments.raw: -afh  passive detection of AFH channel map

commands.name: ubertooth-btle
commands.description: Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) sniffing and more

commands.name: ubertooth-debug
commands.description: Classic Bluetooth discovery, sniffing, and decoding

commands.name: ubertooth-ducky
commands.description: ubertooth-ducky - make an Uberducky quack like a USB Rubber Ducky

commands.name: ubertooth-dump
commands.description: output a continuous stream of received bits

commands.name: ubertooth-follow
commands.description: CLK discovery and follow for a particular UAP/LAP

commands.name: ubertooth-rx
commands.description: Classic Bluetooth discovery, sniffing, and decoding

commands.name: ubertooth-scan
commands.description: Scan frequency band active (Bluez) device scan and inquiry supported by Ubertooth

commands.name: ubertooth-specan-ui
commands.description: spectrum analyzer for the 2.4 GHz ISM band

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ubertooth-afh because 2.4 GHz wireless development platform for Bluetooth experimentation.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ubertooth-afh as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ubertooth-afh -u <uap> -l <lap> [-r] [-m <seconds>]` as documented upstream for ubertooth-afh.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ubertooth-afh with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ubertooth-afh --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -afh
arguments.description: passive detection of AFH channel map
